<!-- Generated by the render-fidelity-cross-reference workflow, 2026-06-01. -->

# Render reconciliation — ours vs orsc vs Jagex (ground truth)

## Status

Across the four adjudicated areas (terrain/water/overlays, walls/scenery/diagonal-doors, roofs/scene/raster, render-diff tooling), the Jagex rev-235 clean decompile (`reference/rsc-client/decompiled/normalized-clean/{k,lb,wb}.java` + the readable deob `src/client/world/World.java`, `src/client/Mudclient.java`, `src/client/scene/GameModel.java`) is the arbiter. The honest tally: **zero** of our render-behavior fixes turned out to be cases where orsc was buggy and we were uniquely right on the *same algorithm* — every one of our four "fidelity bug" fixes (water-flatten-by-tileType, type-2 floor-split gate, split-by-tile-class, overlay neighbour-spread, wall front/back colour table) was repairing **our own prior bug** to reach a parity orsc already had, so those are **REDUNDANT (both correct)**. Where we *are* uniquely correct vs orsc is **three genuine orsc gaps** — the under-roof cull (R1), the id-74 windmill-sail lift, and the entire 48001..59999 diagonal scenery-object/door band (`World.addModels`) — these are the **highest-value forward-ports** because orsc builds wrong or zero geometry there and diverges from both Jagex and its own OpenRSC lineage. Conversely, **two places orsc is uniquely more correct than ours** (multi-story roof elevation-cache anchor R5, roof plane-cap R6) where we used gated single-plane-dump shims — we **ADOPT-ORSC** (and orsc's `stories.go` already implements both faithfully). One behavior pair (bridge 250-remap seam coord; type-5 bridge sentinel; fixed-shade water) is where orsc is more byte-faithful to Jagex but ours is an intentional legibility enhancement — settle by the JAR pixel oracle. Net: **3 orsc-gaps to fill (B), 2 adopt-orsc (C), ~20 redundant-both-correct (D), and ~3 items that NEED-RUNTIME-DIFF (E)** against the rev-235 jar — there are **no (A) "orsc-bug-our-fix-corrects-the-same-algorithm" items**, because our fixes corrected ours, not orsc.

## Consolidated verdict table

Grouped by ACTION. `cite` = Jagex ground-truth location. Files: OURS = working-tree `render/*.go`; ORSC = `origin/main:render/orsc/*.go`.

### (A) FORWARD-PORT-OURS-FIXES-ORSC — orsc-bug-on-same-algorithm our fix corrects (HIGHEST VALUE)

**None.** Every one of our four render-fidelity fixes repaired an *ours-only* prior bug to reach parity orsc already had. There is no item where ours and orsc implement the same algorithm and orsc is wrong. The high-value forward-ports are the **(B) gaps** below — behaviors orsc never implemented at all.

### (B) PORT-OURS-TO-ORSC-GAP — orsc builds wrong/zero geometry; our impl is correct, forward-port it

| behavior | winner | action | Jagex cite | note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Under-roof cull** — hide plane-0 roofs + upper walls/roofs when host tile `objectAdjacency & 0x80` is set | **ours** | PORT-OURS→ORSC | `client.java:6952` / deob `Mudclient.java:7222` `(bb[hx][hy]&128)==0 && yj==0`; bit set `World.java:979` `tileType==2 ⇒ \|=128` | OURS `roof.go:31-34,render.go:208,231` is byte-exact. ORSC `roofs.go` has **no cull** (grep = 0 hits) → bot occluded by its own roof / interior reads dark. Port `HostUnderRoof` + the `!underRoof` gate. |
| **id-74 windmill-sail vertical lift (-480 on Y)** | **ours** | PORT-OURS→ORSC | deob `Mudclient.java:6229-6231` & `4296-4298` `if(objType==74) model.a(0,0,-480,true)`; baseZ→Y via `GameModel.java:838-847,1200-1201` | OURS `scenery.go:147-149` `if DefID==74 { cy -= 480 }`. ORSC `scenery_place.go` / `world.go:659 PlaceScenery` does Orient+Translate only, no per-id lift (grep `74`/`-480` = 0 hits) → sails lie flat. Add the `-480` special-case in `PlaceScenery`. |
| **Diagonal scenery objects/doors band 48001..59999 (`World.addModels`)** | **ours** | PORT-OURS→ORSC | deob `World.java:826-854 addModels`; clean `k.java:1930-2010` (`48000<d<60000`, `objectId=d-48001`, footprint centre, `orient(…,roll=dir*32)`, footprint-clear) | OURS `diagobj.go BuildDiagonalObjects` ports it exactly. ORSC handles only diagonal *walls* `<24000` (`world.go:396,416`); `scenery_place.go:11-13` explicitly skips "48000+ inline markers" for `SceneryLocs.json`. Builds **zero** geometry for the band — diverges from Jagex AND from OpenRSC's own `World.java:168-193`. Port `diagobj.go` driven off the landscape diag grid. |
| (sub of above) diag-object orientation `dir*32 → ROLL` not yaw | ours | (part of diagobj port) | clean `k.java:1960` `g(0,-999999,0,dir*32)` = orient(roll=dir*32) | OUR `Rotate(0,0,dir*32)` matches the clean roll arg. Faithful; ships with the diagobj port. |
| **rscdump/1 shared-state schema** (deterministic 3-engine input) | ours | PORT-OURS→ORSC (tooling) | n/a (apparatus) | `internal/rscdump/{schema.go:51-225,landscape.go:18-63}`. orsc has none (consumes loose `render.View`/ints). `Dump.Landscape()` already yields the `*pathfind.Landscape` orsc.RenderView takes. |

| **Structural face export `[]BuiltFace`** + `Key()`/`CentroidGrid=16` | ours | PORT-OURS→ORSC (tooling) | n/a | `render/dump_faces.go:25-131`. orsc field map is exact: `resetTransformCache→vertXTransform/Y/Z`, `faceIndices[fi]`, `faceTextureFront/Back[fi]`, `faceHead`. Fields unexported ⇒ retarget in-pkg `render/orsc/dump_faces.go` or export `BuildScene`+accessor. Keep `BuiltFace` type stable — JAR oracle hardcodes `grid=16` + field names (`DumpRenderer.java:249,282-284`). |
| **Pixel diff / heatmap / .png decode / face-set asym diff / CLI + sidecar IO + CI gate** | ours | PORT-OURS→ORSC (tooling) | n/a | `cmd/renderdiff/{diff.go:67-225,main.go:57-265}`. Renderer-agnostic — depends only on `render.RenderDump`/`RenderDumpFaces`/`BuiltFace`; repoint those 3 symbols. |
| **terrainSeed ambience RNG-pin** (deterministic byte-exact render) | ours | PORT-OURS→ORSC (tooling) | per-vertex speckle is `(int)(random*10)-5`, `World.java:930-932`/`k.java:703-705` | `render/dump.go:56`. The one renderer-internal piece — must be re-expressed inside orsc `world.go` terrain build or orsc renders are non-reproducible and the pixel diff is noise. |

They re-verify the forward-ports against orsc geometry. |

### (C) ADOPT-ORSC — orsc verified more correct than ours

| **Multi-story roof height anchor** | **orsc** | ADOPT-ORSC | clean `World.java:876-880,820-822`: `tileElevationCache` seeded once (plane-0), never reset; upper floors stack on lower wall-tops | OURS `roof.go:75,210,448` uses a flat `planeWallTopBonus=192` per-plane shim (FINDINGS #12, gated on single-plane dump). | **Roof plane-range cap** | **orsc** | ADOPT-ORSC | clean `World.java:876-880`: build host plane, then `if(plane==0)` build planes 1,2 — **never plane 3** | OURS `roof.go:62,447` `maxRoofPlane=3` builds a plane-3 roof the deob never does, and over-builds upper stories when host≠plane0 (FINDINGS #13). ORSC `stories.go:37-41 storyPlanes(0)={0,1,2}` only when `host==plane0`. Adopt orsc. |
| **Bridge 250-remap seam coord** | **orsc** | ADOPT-ORSC (+NEEDS-RUNTIME-DIFF) | `setTiles World.java:696-708`; `k.java so()` — region-local `x==47`/`y==47` | OURS `terrain.go:117-127` uses WORLD `wx%48==47` (the known `bridge_seam_coord` gap, FINDINGS #15, PROPOSED-unapplied) — wrong columns on a non-sector-aligned window. | **Type-5 bridge-sentinel colour from neighbour decks** | **orsc** | ADOPT-ORSC | clean `World.java:950-965`; `k.java:776-819` | OURS `terrain.go:316-318` keeps grass (no type-5 handling). ORSC `world.go:583-609` ports the neighbour-deck split. Minor (ids 8/10 rare) but adopt for parity. |

OURS builds all ids ungated (FINDINGS #9, intentional-for-bot). Flagged for honesty; not in scope, OURS left as-is. |

### (E) NEEDS-RUNTIME-DIFF — source reading cannot settle; settle vs rev-235 jar

| behavior | winner | Jagex cite | note |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Water rendered as fixed-shade textured quad + shoreline diagonal** | orsc (faithfulness) vs ours (legibility) | `World.java:947-949` emits `colour=1` via the **normal gouraud `createFace` path** `:987-1015`; no fixed shade, no shoreline branch (`k.java:767-773,820-879`) | OURS `terrain.go:49-64,288-296,362-377` deliberately diverges: `AddFixedFace` + `waterShade=40` bright fixed shade + invented shoreline-corner branch (`bridge_shoreline_bleed`, FINDINGS #16, no deob counterpart). ORSC `world.go:574-581` matches Jagex exactly. OUR divergence is an intentional legibility choice (water never gouraud-darkens to a black cliff), **not** a bug to forward-port. If pixel-fidelity to JAR is required, adopt orsc's plain colour=1 path → JAR oracle decides. |
| Texture projection shift `vp` (8 vs 9) | agree (ours==orsc=9); arbiter ambiguous | clean ctor seed `lb.java:4681 R=8`; settable `R=var6` `lb.java:3802`; runtime override = setMidpoints `m_qd=9` | Two independent ports both resolved vp=9 (strong corroboration of the runtime override over the ctor's 8). They cannot disagree; byte-pin 8-vs-9 against a JAR frame. |
| Painter plane-pierce `polygonHit1/2` byte-level | agree (structure matches) | clean `lb.java` overlap family | Structure matches; full byte confirmation deferred to runtime diff. |
| orsc-vs-Jagex runtime pixel/struct agreement (whole renderer) | unknown | rev-235 jar via `DumpRenderer` (arbiter, not yet run) | deob is not yet a runtime oracle (DEOB_GAPS §1: 10 behavioral bugs + compile gate). Settle once faces/CLI legs are retargeted onto orsc and fed to the JAR oracle. |

## Integration plan (work-preserving)

### Forward-ports — our findings that become orsc fixes (with the orsc file:line to change)

1. **Under-roof cull (R1) → `render/orsc/roofs.go`.** Add a `HostUnderRoof` predicate computed from the host tile's `objectAdjacency & 0x80` (set when `tileType==2`, per `World.java:979`) and gate roof/upper-wall emission on `!underRoof`, mirroring OURS `roof.go:31-34` + `render.go:208,231`. orsc currently has zero cull.
2. **id-74 lift → `render/orsc/world.go` `PlaceScenery` (line 659).** Add `if defID == 74 { cy -= 480 }` before/within the Translate, per `Mudclient.java:6229-6231`. Today `PlaceScenery` does Orient+Translate only.
3. **Diagonal scenery objects/doors 48001..59999 → new `render/orsc/diagobj.go`** (port of OURS `diagobj.go`), driven off the landscape diag grid (not `SceneryLocs.json`), wired into `BuildStories`/the scene build. orsc's `world.go` only consumes the diag grid for `<24000` walls (`:396,:416`); `scenery_place.go:11-13` explicitly declines the 48000+ band. Use `dir*32→roll` (`Rotate(0,0,dir*32)`), footprint-centre translate, `-elevation` snap, local footprint-clear.

### orsc gaps to fill — tooling (one-time mechanical retarget)

4. **`render/orsc/dump_faces.go`** (new, in-package): port `sceneFaces` to walk orsc `Scene.models`, bake via `(*Model).resetTransformCache()`, read `vertXTransform/YTransform/ZTransform`, `faceIndices[fi]`, `faceTextureFront/Back[fi]`, `faceHead`. Either keep it in-package (fields unexported) or export a `BuildScene(land,f,v) *Scene` + a `(*Model)` face accessor. Keep `render.BuiltFace`/`Key()`/`CentroidGrid=16` types stable — the JAR oracle hardcodes them.
5. **`render/orsc` dump→PNG+buffer leg**: add a sibling to `RenderView` (`view.go:36`) that also returns `surf.Pixels []int32` (byte-identical to `render.Surface.Pix`); wire `RenderDump` to `Dump.Landscape() → orsc.RenderView`.
6. **terrainSeed RNG-pin**: re-express `withDumpTerrainSeed` (`dump.go:56`) inside orsc `world.go` terrain build, else renders are non-reproducible and the pixel diff is noise.
7. **`cmd/renderdiff`**: repoint the 3 symbols `RenderDump`/`RenderDumpFaces`/`BuiltFace` at the orsc-backed equivalents (or a thin renderdiff-internal adapter). The pixel diff, heatmap, .png decode, structural face-set diff, sidecar IO, and CI exit gate are unchanged.
8. **`render/fidelity_*_test.go`**: same one-line symbol swap as `dump_faces.go`; **do not delete** — they regress-guard the forward-ports (1-3) against orsc geometry.

### Adopt-orsc (already implemented in orsc — verify, don't re-port)

- R5 multi-story anchor: orsc `stories.go:50-54` already threads the un-reset `elevCache`. R6 plane-cap: `stories.go:37-41 storyPlanes`. Bridge seam coord: `world.go:247-315 setTileDecorationOnBridge`. Type-5 sentinel: `world.go:583-609`. Drop OURS' `planeWallTopBonus`/`maxRoofPlane=3`/`wx%48==47` shims — orsc supersedes them.

### PRESERVED-ARTIFACTS (the rebase MUST keep — none of these are render-source edits)

- All docs under `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/docs/build/`: `RENDER_FIDELITY_FINDINGS.md`, `RENDER_DEOB_GAPS.md`, `RENDER_DIFF_DESIGN.md`, `RENDER_DIFF_STATUS.md`, `UPSTREAM_RENDER_DELTA.md`, `REBASE_PLAN.md`, `GO_JAR_DIFF_RESULTS.md`, `findings/`, plus `MILESTONE_*`, `NET_HANDLER_AUDIT.md`, `OBF_MEMBER_MAP.tsv`, `OPTIONS.md`, `NAMING*.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `MUDCLIENT_SKELETON.md`.
- All fixtures: `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/testdata/rscdump/**` (incl. `single_tile_door.json`, `single_tile_NOdoor.json`, `hunt/`, `out/`).
- The schema package: `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/internal/rscdump/` (`schema.go`, `landscape.go`, `gen/`).
- The CLI: `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/cmd/renderdiff/` (`main.go`, `diff.go`, `README.md`, `selftest_test.go`).
- The JAR oracle (separate repo, schema-coupled, untouched): `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/rscplus/dumprender/DumpRenderer.java` (+ `*Probe.java`, `PatchAndRender.java`).
- The deob/clean arbiter (untouched): `reference/rsc-client/src/client/**`, `reference/rsc-client/decompiled/normalized-clean/**`.

## Revised rebase approach

The old `REBASE_PLAN.md` "drop the 3 render commits" is too blunt — it would discard the dump schema, the CLI, the JAR oracle, the fixtures, and the documented fixes. Instead, **keep all non-render content and only supersede the old-render `.go` source edits**, then land the forward-ports/gap-fills as fresh commits on the orsc base. On `origin/main` the old monolithic renderer files (`render/{terrain,boundary,roof,scenery,diagobj,scene,model,rasterize,dump,dump_faces}.go` + `render/fidelity_*_test.go`) are **already deleted** and replaced by `render/orsc/*` plus a slim shared `render/{render,entity,entitysprite,itemsprite,sprites,animdefs,composite_export,playerappearance_test}.go`. Concrete sequence:

1. **Branch off `origin/main`** (the orsc base) — `git checkout -b render/reconcile-orsc origin/main`. Do NOT rebase the 3 render commits onto it.
2. **Cherry-pick / re-apply the NON-render content** from our branch as-is: the entire `internal/rscdump/`, `cmd/renderdiff/`, `testdata/rscdump/**`, and all `docs/build/**` docs. These don't touch `render/*.go` and apply cleanly. (`24495c5` is mostly these + the now-superseded old-API `render/dump*.go`; take the schema/CLI/fixtures/docs portions, drop the old-API `render/*.go` portions.)
3. **Supersede the old-render source**: do NOT re-introduce any of the upstream-deleted `render/*.go` files. The forward-ported *behaviors* now live in orsc, not in resurrected old files.
4. **Land forward-port commit 1** — `feat(render/orsc): under-roof cull + id-74 sail lift + diagonal-object band (48001..59999)`: edits `render/orsc/roofs.go`, `render/orsc/world.go`, new `render/orsc/diagobj.go`, each cited to the Jagex source in this doc.
5. **Land tooling-retarget commit 2** — `feat(render/orsc): dump/faces/pixel-CLI retarget onto orsc renderer`: new `render/orsc/dump_faces.go` + raw-buffer `RenderView` sibling + terrainSeed RNG-pin in `world.go`; repoint `cmd/renderdiff` + retarget `render/fidelity_*_test.go`.
6. **Adopt-orsc is a no-op** (orsc already implements R5/R6/seam/type-5) — just delete OURS' shims by virtue of building on orsc; note in the commit message that FINDINGS #12/#13/#15 are resolved by orsc's architecture.
7. **Run the regression guards**: `go test ./render/... ./cmd/renderdiff/...` — the retargeted fidelity tests now re-verify the forward-ports against orsc geometry. Nothing of value is lost; where we were more correct, orsc gets our fix; where orsc was more correct, we drop our shim.

## Open items — what the runtime JAR-oracle diff (orsc vs rev-235 jar) must settle

1. **Water rendering (E):** does the JAR draw water via the plain gouraud `colour=1` path (orsc, faithful) or does OUR fixed-shade+shoreline enhancement read closer to the live client's intent? Pixel-diff a water/bridge tile fixture; this is the one deliberate ours-vs-Jagex divergence and the JAR is the only arbiter that can rule on legibility-vs-fidelity.
2. **Texture projection `vp` = 8 vs 9 (R9):** both ports resolved 9 (runtime `m_qd` override over the ctor `R=8`); byte-pin against a JAR frame to confirm the override fires before render.
3. **Bridge 250-remap seam (C):** confirm orsc's region-local `x==47`/`y==47` lands the plank lip on the right columns vs OURS' world `wx%48==47` on a **sector-aligned** dump (the bug only manifests off a sector boundary).
4. **Painter plane-pierce `polygonHit1/2` (E):** structure matches; byte-confirm the plane-pierce branch on an overlapping-face fixture.
5. **Whole-renderer orsc-vs-jar agreement:** with `dump_faces.go` + the CLI retargeted onto orsc, run `DumpRenderer.java` over the `testdata/rscdump/**` fixtures to produce `rscdump-faces/1` + PNG, then `renderdiff` orsc-vs-jar. This is the first time the deob/jar arbiter is exercised at runtime (DEOB_GAPS §1 notes the deob is not yet a trustworthy oracle), and it settles every remaining E-row at once.
